title: 1: Code Maintainability

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 1.1: Removes both distutils imports (archive_util, setup) from setup.py

annotations.description: Removes all imports from distutils (e.g. distutils.core, distutils.archive_util) so that setup.py no longer depends on the deprecated distutils module.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 195-199: removes 'from distutils import archive_util' and the 'from distutils.core import setup' fallback.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'require setuptools instead of falling back to distutils'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] distutils is deprecated per Python 3.10+. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must remove all distutils imports from setup.py.

title: 1.2: Uses setuptools for package configuration in setup.py

annotations.description: Imports and uses setuptools (e.g. from setuptools import setup) as the sole packaging backend in setup.py.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 200: 'from setuptools import setup, find_packages'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'require setuptools instead of falling back to distutils'. [3: Logical Necessity] After removing distutils, setuptools must be used as the packaging backend. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must use setuptools for package configuration.

title: 1.3: Uses automatic package discovery via find_packages() for package configuration in setup.py

annotations.description: Replaces the manually enumerated packages list in setup() with an automatic discovery mechanism (e.g. find_packages()).
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 281: 'packages=find_packages()' replaces the hardcoded list of 18 sub-packages removed at lines 258-279.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'use automatic package discovery instead of a hardcoded list'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must replace the hardcoded packages list with automatic discovery.

title: 1.4: Defines an entry point that auto-generates an executable for the scapy interactive shell in setup.py

annotations.description: Adds an entry point in setup() that auto-generates an executable for the scapy interactive shell, targeting scapy.main:interact.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 287-289: entry_points with 'scapy = scapy.main:interact'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'replace the manual script installation with console script entry points that auto-generate executables for Scapy's interactive shell'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must define an entry point for the scapy interactive shell.

title: 1.5: Defines an entry point that auto-generates an executable for the UTscapy test runner in setup.py

annotations.description: Adds an entry point in setup() that auto-generates an executable for the UTscapy test runner, targeting scapy.tools.UTscapy:main.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 287-291: entry_points with 'UTscapy = scapy.tools.UTscapy:main'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'auto-generate executables for... its test runner UTscapy'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must define an entry point for the UTscapy test runner.

title: 1.6: Removes the deprecated scripts parameter from setup() in setup.py

annotations.description: Removes the SCRIPTS variable and the scripts= keyword argument from the setup() call, since executables are now generated via entry_points.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 249-252: removes SCRIPTS=['bin/scapy', 'bin/UTscapy'] and the Windows extension; line 280: removes scripts=SCRIPTS.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to replace manual script installation with entry points. [3: Logical Necessity] Using both scripts= and entry_points= would create duplicate executables. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must remove the scripts parameter from setup().

title: 1.7: Removes legacy ezip archive building code from setup.py

annotations.description: Removes the EZIP_HEADER constant, the make_ezipfile function, and the archive format registration from setup.py.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 207-233: removes EZIP_HEADER, make_ezipfile(), and the ARCHIVE_FORMATS['ezip'] registration.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The legacy executable ZIP building code... should be removed entirely'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must remove all ezip-related code from setup.py.

title: 1.8: Declares supported Python versions in setup.py

annotations.description: Adds a declaration to setup() specifying which Python versions are supported (e.g. python_requires).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The setup configuration should also declare which Python versions are supported'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must declare supported Python versions in setup.py.

title: 1.9: Defines optional dependency groups in setup.py

annotations.description: Adds optional dependency groups in setup() for enhanced features such as interactive shells, plotting, and cryptography (e.g. extras_require).
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 294-304: extras_require with 'basic' (ipython) and 'complete' (ipython, pyx, cryptography, matplotlib) groups.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'define optional dependency groups for users who want extras like enhanced interactive shells, plotting, and cryptography support'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must define optional dependency groups in setup.py.

title: 1.10: Changes UTscapy main() signature to accept no required arguments in scapy/tools/UTscapy.py

annotations.description: Modifies the main function in scapy/tools/UTscapy.py so it can be invoked with no arguments, reading sys.argv internally.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 168-170: changes 'def main(argv):' to 'def main():' and adds 'argv = sys.argv[1:]' inside the function body.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Update main so it can be invoked without arguments'. The interface specifies Input: NA. [3: Logical Necessity] Setuptools console_scripts call entry point functions with no arguments. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must make main() callable without required arguments.

title: 1.11: Updates both call sites of main() in scapy/tools/UTscapy.py (res=main(), sys.exit(main())) to pass no arguments

annotations.description: Updates all existing calls to main() within scapy/tools/UTscapy.py to invoke main() without positional arguments.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 178-179: changes 'res = main(sys.argv[1:])' to 'res = main()'; lines 184-185: changes 'sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))' to 'sys.exit(main())'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'update its call sites within the file accordingly'. [3: Logical Necessity] After making main() handle argv internally, callers must stop passing argv. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must update all internal calls to main() to pass no arguments.

title: 1.12: Makes run_scapy.bat a standalone canonical launcher defaulting to Python 3

annotations.description: Refactors run_scapy.bat to be a self-contained launcher that defaults to Python 3, instead of delegating to run_scapy_py2.bat and run_scapy_py3.bat.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 101-116: replaces the old delegation to py2/py3 batch files with standalone logic that sets PYTHON=python3 and falls back to python.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'run_scapy.bat serves as the single canonical launcher that defaults to Python 3 and falls back if needed'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must make run_scapy.bat a standalone launcher defaulting to Python 3.

title: 1.13: Simplifies run_scapy_py2.bat to delegate to run_scapy.bat

annotations.description: Reduces run_scapy_py2.bat to set the Python interpreter variable and call run_scapy.bat, removing its own launch logic.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 123-137: replaces 13 lines of duplicated launch logic with 'set PYTHON=python' and 'call run_scapy.bat'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the per-version batch files simply set the interpreter and delegate to it'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must simplify run_scapy_py2.bat to delegate to run_scapy.bat.

title: 1.14: Simplifies run_scapy_py3.bat to delegate to run_scapy.bat

annotations.description: Reduces run_scapy_py3.bat to set the Python 3 interpreter variable and call run_scapy.bat, removing its own launch logic.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 143-158: replaces 13 lines of duplicated launch logic with 'set PYTHON=python3' and 'call run_scapy.bat'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the per-version batch files simply set the interpreter and delegate to it'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must simplify run_scapy_py3.bat to delegate to run_scapy.bat.

title: 1.15: Defaults run_scapy shell script to Python 3

annotations.description: Updates the run_scapy Unix shell script to default the PYTHON variable to python3 instead of python.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 94: changes 'PYTHON=${PYTHON:-python}' to 'PYTHON=${PYTHON:-python3}'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The Unix run_scapy shell script should also default to Python 3'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must change the default from python to python3 in run_scapy.

title: 1.16: Deletes the bin/scapy wrapper script

annotations.description: Removes the bin/scapy file since executables are now auto-generated via entry points.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 44-74: deletes bin/scapy (deleted file mode).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The... wrapper scripts in bin/ should be removed entirely'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must remove bin/scapy.

annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The... wrapper scripts in bin/ should be removed entirely'. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must remove bin/UTscapy.

title: 4.1: Breaks the scapy interactive shell launch capability

annotations.description: Checks that the scapy interactive shell can still be launched after refactoring, whether via entry points, run_scapy scripts, or python -m scapy.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 289: 'scapy = scapy.main:interact' entry point preserves shell launch. run_scapy and run_scapy.bat still invoke 'python -m scapy'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for auto-generated executables for the interactive shell, implying it must remain functional. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must not break the scapy shell launch capability.

title: 4.2: Breaks the UTscapy test runner launch capability

annotations.description: Checks that the UTscapy test runner can still be launched after refactoring, whether via entry points or python -m scapy.tools.UTscapy.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 290: 'UTscapy = scapy.tools.UTscapy:main' entry point. Lines 169-170: main() now reads sys.argv internally, compatible with entry point invocation.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for auto-generated executables for the test runner. The interface specifies main() with Input: NA. [5: Conclusion] Any correct answer must not break the UTscapy test runner launch capability.
